evals.prompt: How do I create a typed reactive form in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use FormGroup<T> with a typed interface: FormGroup<{ email: FormControl<string>; password: FormControl<string> }>. Use FormBuilder with nonNullable: true to avoid null in form values. Never use untyped FormGroup.

evals.prompt: How do I use FormBuilder to create a FormGroup?
evals.expected_output: Inject FormBuilder with fb = inject(FormBuilder). Use fb.nonNullable.group({ email: ['', Validators.email], password: ['', Validators.required] }). This creates typed controls with nonNullable values.

evals.prompt: How do I write a custom validator for an Angular form?
evals.expected_output: Create a standalone validator function: function passwordStrength(control: AbstractControl): ValidationErrors | null { ... }. Pass to Validators array. Extract into a separate file — do not put validation logic inside the component class.

evals.prompt: How do I use nonNullable reactive forms in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use FormBuilder.nonNullable: const form = fb.nonNullable.group({ name: '' }). This ensures form.value returns string instead of string | null. Use nonNullable: true option on individual FormControl for fine-grained control.

assertions.value: returns string instead of string | null

evals.prompt: Should I use template-driven or reactive forms in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use Reactive Forms (FormControl, FormGroup) for any non-trivial inputs. Template-driven forms are only acceptable for very simple, single-field cases. Reactive forms are strictly typed, testable, and compose better with Signal stores.

evals.prompt: How do I sync a FormGroup with an Angular Signal Store?
evals.expected_output: Use valueChanges.pipe(takeUntilDestroyed()) to subscribe to form changes and call store.update(). Or use effect() watching a signal to patch the form. Keep form logic in the component and validation in standalone validator functions.

should_not_trigger: How do I build a controlled form in React with useState?
